name: Larsen Jay
bio: Two-term Knox County At-Large Commissioner and former Commission Chairman. Founded Random Acts of Flowers, a national nonprofit. Largest fundraising total in Knox County mayoral history.

early_childhood_ed.summary: Supports fully funding public schools; no specific early childhood or pre-K policy articulated.
early_childhood_ed.details: Jay lists fully funding the public school system as his third top priority, stating 'there's no better investment than in education.' He has served on the Knox Education Foundation board. His focus is on K-12 investment and the county's overall education budget rather than a distinct early childhood initiative.

child_hunger.summary: No public position found on child hunger or food insecurity specifically.
child_hunger.details: Jay's campaign platform does not address child hunger, school meal programs, or food insecurity as standalone issues. He broadly emphasizes strengthening the county's quality of life and health services.

parental_support.summary: Emphasizes strong infrastructure and public services as support for families; no explicit childcare or family leave policy.
parental_support.details: Jay frames his candidacy around ensuring Knox County remains a place where 'our kids and grandkids can live here.' He champions road safety, school funding, and law enforcement as pillars of family support. No specific childcare subsidy or parental support program proposals found.

name: Betsy Henderson
bio: Two-term Knox County School Board member elected Board Chair twice. Former staffer for Congressman Jimmy Duncan. Emphasizes parental rights, low taxes, and conservative Christian values.

early_childhood_ed.summary: Focuses on K-12 accountability and school choice; supported private school voucher expansion; no specific pre-K policy.
early_childhood_ed.details: Henderson touts her school board record including Knox County Schools being recognized as an 'Advancing' district. She testified in favor of Governor Lee's school choice voucher expansion in January 2025. She does not articulate a specific early childhood or pre-K platform.

child_hunger.summary: No public position found on child hunger or food insecurity specifically.
child_hunger.details: Henderson's platform centers on fiscal restraint and government staying within core functions (roads, schools, public safety). No specific child nutrition stance was found.

parental_support.summary: Strong champion of parental rights in education; fought to keep schools open during COVID; supported school voucher expansion.
parental_support.details: Henderson frames her candidacy around 'empowering parents to make the best decisions for their children.' She fought to keep schools open during COVID, opposed mask mandates, and led efforts to keep law enforcement in Knox County schools. Her broader parental support stance ties to low-tax pledges.

name: Kim Frazier
bio: Knox County At-Large Commissioner and Vice Chair. Former nonprofit development consultant and small business owner. Co-founded the Knox Community Planning Alliance. Self-funded ~77% of campaign.

early_childhood_ed.summary: Supports strong schools and expanded career pathways for youth; no specific pre-K policy articulated.
early_childhood_ed.details: Frazier lists 'Strong Education and Career Pathways' as a core priority, citing her record on school capacity and teacher raises. She does not articulate a specific early childhood initiative.

child_hunger.summary: No public position found on child hunger or food insecurity.
child_hunger.details: Frazier's 'Mental and Physical Health, Families, and Community Well-Being' priority area supports expanding the Knox County Health Department but does not specifically address child hunger or food insecurity.

parental_support.summary: Emphasizes community-first leadership and essential services for families; no specific childcare or family leave policy.
parental_support.details: Frazier's civic engagement started from concern for her sons' overcrowded school. Her platform includes health and family well-being as a category and she supports the Health Department's prevention-focused programming.

name: Beau Hawk
bio: President of the Knoxville-Oak Ridge Central Labor Council representing 30+ unions. Ironworker raised by a single mother. Sole Democrat in the race; faces Republican primary winner in August.

early_childhood_ed.summary: Supports fully funding public schools and expanding workforce training; no specific pre-K policy.
early_childhood_ed.details: Hawk lists workforce development and vocational training as a top priority. He broadly supports education investment, framing it through economic opportunity and working-family needs. No specific early childhood program proposal found.

child_hunger.summary: No explicit child hunger position; broader platform addresses working-family affordability.
child_hunger.details: Hawk's campaign centers on affordability for working families, noting 'housing costs are rising' and 'working families are being squeezed.' His background representing grocery store workers gives him a connection to economic hardship, but no specific child nutrition policy was found.

parental_support.summary: Focuses on working-family affordability, housing, and good-paying jobs as the foundation of parental support.
parental_support.details: Hawk frames his candidacy around making Knox County 'a place where families can afford to stay.' He was raised by a single mother, a narrative he invokes to signal his commitment to working families. His labor background suggests alignment with worker-family benefits but no specific proposals found.
